own_review_points: AbstractTransactSQLDialect registers JsonAsStringJdbcType.NVARCHAR_INSTANCE / XmlAsStringJdbcType.NVARCHAR_INSTANCE unconditionally, regardless of use_nationalized_character_data
jTDS AbstractMethodError fallback hardcodes StandardCharsets.UTF_8 for byte<->String — corrupts data if the Sybase DB uses a different encoding
jTDS/Sybase AbstractMethodError fallback paths are entirely untested (tests gate on SupportsNationalizedData, never the jTDS driver)
AbstractMethodError catch blocks are dead code for SQL Server's official Microsoft driver (base class serves both SQLServer and Sybase)
Array variants (JsonAsStringArrayJdbcType/XmlAsStringArrayJdbcType) get the jTDS fallback but no NVARCHAR_INSTANCE registration — coverage gap
PR description says 'Added a descriptor to DdlTypeRegistry' but the code uses JdbcTypeRegistry.addDescriptor
Human: formatting (spaces around parens and ternary ?/:) and collapsing the byte[]/wrap lines
notes: Six grounded good points: PR-description/registry mismatch (verified getJdbcTypeRegistry()), unconditional NVARCHAR registration, hardcoded-UTF_8 corruption risk, dead AbstractMethodError catch for the MS driver, untested jTDS fallback, and uncovered array variants. One WRONG (P4): v8 claims the base ResultSet.doExtract fallback still passes raw bytes while only the CallableStatement path converts to UTF-8 ('inconsistent') — but the diff shows the ResultSet path was ALSO converted to UTF-8 (NVarcharJdbcType), so they are consistent; the claim is refuted (not a fabrication about absent code, just a misread). Missed the human's formatting theme.
